description_long: This object is a classic fire hydrant, typically found in urban environments for emergency water access. It features a cylindrical red body with a domed top and two side outlets for hose connections, as well as a central front-facing outlet. The hydrant is capped with a prominent operating nut on the top, which is used to open the water valve. The surface appears to be made of painted metal, giving it a hard and durable exterior suitable for outdoor use. No visible chains are attached to the caps, and there are no discernible manufacturer markings. The hydrant is compact and robust, designed to withstand harsh weather and physical impacts.
description: A red, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top, three hose outlets, and a central operating nut, made of painted metal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet and the operating nut on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the side hose outlets and the cylindrical body.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the hydrant with the domed top and no visible outlets.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the other side hose outlet visible.
